{{Short description|US film and TV award ceremony in 2019}} {{Use mdy dates|date=September 2022}} {{Infobox film awards | number = 76 | award = Golden Globe Awards | image = 76th Golden Globe Awards.png{{!}}border | caption = | date = January 6, 2019 | site = [[The Beverly Hilton]],
[[Beverly Hills, California]], U.S. | host = [[Sandra Oh]]
[[Andy Samberg]]{{cite news |last1=Andreeva |first1=Nellie |title=Golden Globes: Andy Samberg & Sandra Oh To Co-Host 2019 Awards On NBC |url=https://deadline.com/2018/12/golden-globes-andy-samberg-sandra-oh-co-host-2019-awards-nbc-1202514404/ |access-date=December 6, 2018 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=December 5, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181206122628/https://deadline.com/2018/12/golden-globes-andy-samberg-sandra-oh-co-host-2019-awards-nbc-1202514404/ |archive-date=December 6, 2018 |url-status=live}} | best_film_drama = ''[[Bohemian Rhapsody (film)|Bohemian Rhapsody]]'' | best_film_musical_or_comedy = ''[[Green Book (film)|Green Book]]'' | best_drama_series = ''[[The Americans]]'' | best_musical_or_comedy_series = ''[[The Kominsky Method]]'' | best_miniseries_or_tv_movie = ''[[The Assassination of Gianni Versace: American Crime Story]]'' | most_wins = ''Green Book'' (3) | most_nominations = ''[[Vice (2018 film)|Vice]]'' (6) | network = [[NBC]] | last = [[75th Golden Globe Awards|75th]] | next = [[77th Golden Globe Awards|77th]] }} The 76th ceremony of the [[Golden Globe Awards]] honored the best in [[2018 in film|film]] and [[2018 in American television|American television of 2018]], as chosen by the [[Hollywood Foreign Press Association]] (HFPA). Produced by [[Dick Clark Productions]] and the HFPA, the ceremony was broadcast live on January 6, 2019, from [[the Beverly Hilton]] in [[Beverly Hills, California]] beginning at 5:00 p.m. [[Pacific Time Zone|PST]] / 8:00 p.m. [[Eastern Time Zone|EST]].{{cite news |last1=Andreeva |first1=Nellie |title=NBC To Remain Golden Globes Home With New 8-Year Deal |url=https://deadline.com/2018/09/nbc-golden-globes-8-year-deal-1202464911/ |access-date=December 2, 2018 |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=September 14, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181203055428/https://deadline.com/2018/09/nbc-golden-globes-8-year-deal-1202464911/ |archive-date=December 3, 2018 |url-status=live}} The ceremony aired live on [[NBC]] in the United States. Actors [[Sandra Oh]] and [[Andy Samberg]] hosted the ceremony. The nominees were announced on December 6, 2018, by [[Terry Crews]], [[Danai Gurira]], [[Leslie Mann]], and [[Christian Slater]].{{cite news |last1=Rubin |first1=Rebecca |title=Presenters Announced for Thursday's Golden Globe Nominations |url=https://variety.com/2018/film/news/terry-crews-leslie-mann-danai-gurira-christian-slater-to-announce-golden-globe-nominations-1203079480/ |access-date=December 4, 2018 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=December 4, 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181204235044/https://variety.com/2018/film/news/terry-crews-leslie-mann-danai-gurira-christian-slater-to-announce-golden-globe-nominations-1203079480/ |archive-date=December 4, 2018 |url-status=live}} The ceremony marked the debut of a new non-competitive award rewarding excellence in television, the Carol Burnett Award, with [[Carol Burnett]] herself being the inaugural recipient.[https://www.goldenglobes.com/articles/carol-burnett-receive-hfpas-first-award-achievement-tv Carol Burnett to Receive HFPA's First Award for Achievement in TV] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181218010627/https://www.goldenglobes.com/articles/carol-burnett-receive-hfpas-first-award-achievement-tv |date=December 18, 2018}}. ''[[Green Book (film)|Green Book]]'' won the most awards for the ceremony with three, including [[Golden Globe Award for Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy|Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy]]. ''[[Bohemian Rhapsody (film)|Bohemian Rhapsody]]'' and ''[[Roma (2018 film)|Roma]]'' won two awards each.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2019/jan/06/golden-globes-2019-live-latest-news-red-carpet-winners-list-speeches|title=Bohemian Rhapsody and Green Book defy odds at Golden Globes 2019 – as it happened|date=January 7, 2019|work=Guardian|access-date=January 8, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190107234414/https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2019/jan/06/golden-globes-2019-live-latest-news-red-carpet-winners-list-speeches|archive-date=January 7, 2019|url-status=live}} In television, ''[[The Kominsky Method]]'' and ''[[The Assassination of Gianni Versace: American Crime Story]]'' were the most awarded, with two awards each.{{cite news|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2019/01/06/movies/golden-globes.html|title=Golden Globes 2019: 'Bohemian Rhapsody' and 'Green Book' Share Spotlight|last=Barnes|first=Brooks|date=January 6, 2018|work=The New York Times|access-date=January 7, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190106232907/https://www.nytimes.com/2019/01/06/movies/golden-globes.html|archive-date=January 6, 2019|url-status=live}} [[Jeff Bridges]] was honored with the [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] for career achievement.{{cite web|url=https://www.cnn.com/2019/01/06/entertainment/jeff-bridges-cecil-b-demille-award/index.html|title=Jeff Bridges accepts Cecil B. DeMille Award|website=CNN|date=January 7, 2019 |language=en|access-date=January 7, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190107040301/https://www.cnn.com/2019/01/06/entertainment/jeff-bridges-cecil-b-demille-award/index.html|archive-date=January 7, 2019|url-status=live}} scope="col" style="width:55px;"| Wins ! scope="col" style="text-align:center;"| Series |- | rowspan="2" style="text-align:center | 2 || ''[[The Assassination of Gianni Versace: American Crime Story]]'' |- | ''[[The Kominsky Method]]'' |} ===Cecil B. DeMille Award=== The [[Golden Globe Cecil B. DeMille Award|Cecil B. DeMille Award]] is an honorary award bestowed for outstanding contributions to the world of entertainment. It is awarded to honorees who have made a significant mark in the film industry and is named after its first recipient, director [[Cecil B. DeMille]]. * [[Jeff Bridges]]{{cite web|url=https://www.goldenglobes.com/articles/jeff-bridges-recipient-cecil-b-demille-award-2019|title=Jeff Bridges: Recipient of the Cecil B. deMille Award 2019|website=www.goldenglobes.com|date=December 17, 2018 |access-date=December 17, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181218010616/https://www.goldenglobes.com/articles/jeff-bridges-recipient-cecil-b-demille-award-2019|archive-date=December 18, 2018|url-status=live}} ===Carol Burnett Award=== The [[Carol Burnett Award]] was first awarded at this ceremony and is an honorary award given for outstanding and lasting contributions to television on or off the screen. It is named in honor of its first recipient. * [[Carol Burnett]] ===Golden Globe Ambassador=== The [[Golden Globe Ambassador]] is a young person, generally a celebrity's daughter or son, who assists in the awards presentations. * Isan Elba (daughter of [[Idris Elba]]){{cite web|url=https://www.goldenglobes.com/articles/isan-elba-2019-golden-globe-ambassador|title=Isan Elba is the 2019 Golden Globe Ambassador|website=www.goldenglobes.com|date=November 14, 2018 |access-date=December 17, 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181207045614/https://www.goldenglobes.com/articles/isan-elba-2019-golden-globe-ambassador|archive-date=December 7, 2018|url-status=live}} ==Ceremony== ===Presenters=== The following individuals presented awards at the ceremony:{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2019/01/golden-globes-golden-globe-awards-presenters-dick-van-dyke-chadwick-boseman-halle-berry-1202528470/|title=Golden Globes: Dick Van Dyke, Chadwick Boseman, Halle Berry Among First Announced Presenters|work=Deadline|last=Evans|first=Greg|date=January 3, 2019|access-date=January 3, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190104021446/https://deadline.com/2019/01/golden-globes-golden-globe-awards-presenters-dick-van-dyke-chadwick-boseman-halle-berry-1202528470/|archive-date=January 4, 2019|url-status=live}}{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2019/01/golden-globes-bradley-cooper-lady-gaga-presenters-taron-egerton-steve-carell-1202528985/|title=Golden Globes: Bradley Cooper & Lady Gaga Set As Presenters; Taron Egerton, Steve Carell Also On Deck|work=Deadline|last=Evans|first=Greg|date=January 4, 2019|access-date=January 4, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190104231028/https://deadline.com/2019/01/golden-globes-bradley-cooper-lady-gaga-presenters-taron-egerton-steve-carell-1202528985/|archive-date=January 4, 2019|url-status=live}} * [[Bradley Cooper]] and [[Lady Gaga]] with Best Actor – Television Series Musical or Comedy * [[Chadwick Boseman]], [[Danai Gurira]], [[Michael B. Jordan]], and [[Lupita Nyong'o]] with Best Animated Feature Film and introduced ''Black Panther'' * [[Olivia Colman]], [[Emma Stone]], and [[Rachel Weisz]] introduced ''The Favourite'' * [[Kaley Cuoco]], [[Johnny Galecki]], and [[Jim Parsons]] with Best Actor – Television Series Drama and Best Television Series – Drama * [[Adam Driver]] and [[John David Washington]] introduced ''BlacKkKlansman'' * [[Taraji P. Henson]] and [[Gina Rodriguez]] with Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film * [[Jamie Lee Curtis]] and [[Ben Stiller]] with Best Actress – Miniseries or Television Film * [[Lucy Liu]] introduced ''Crazy Rich Asians'' * [[Steve Carell]] with the Carol Burnett Award * [[Idris Elba]] and [[Taylor Swift]] with Best Original Score and Best Original Song * [[Octavia Spencer]] introduced ''Green Book'' * [[Allison Janney]] and [[Sam Rockwell]] with Best Supporting Actress – Motion Picture * [[Kristen Bell]] and [[Megan Mullally]] with Best Actress – Television Series Drama * [[Amy Poehler]] and [[Maya Rudolph]] with Best Supporting Actor – Motion Picture and Best Screenplay * [[Felicity Huffman]] and [[William H. Macy]] with Best Supporting Actress – Series, Miniseries or Television Film * [[Saoirse Ronan]] with Best Actor – Motion Picture Musical or Comedy * [[Antonio Banderas]] and [[Catherine Zeta-Jones]] with Best Foreign Language Film * [[Tyler Perry]] introduced ''Vice'' * [[Taron Egerton]] and [[Amber Heard]] with Best Actor – Miniseries or Television Film * [[Chris Pine]] with the Cecil B. DeMille Award * [[Harrison Ford]] with Best Director * [[Sam Elliott]] introduced ''A Star Is Born'' * [[Sterling K. Brown]], [[Justin Hartley]], and [[Chrissy Metz]] with Best Actress – Television Series Musical or Comedy and Best Television Series – Musical or Comedy * [[Emily Blunt]] and [[Dick Van Dyke]] introduced ''Mary Poppins Returns'' * [[Halle Berry]] and [[Lena Waithe]] with Best Miniseries or Television Film * [[Jessica Chastain]] and [[Anne Hathaway]] with Best Actress – Motion Picture Musical or Comedy * [[Janelle Monáe]] introduced ''If Beale Street Could Talk'' * [[Mike Myers]] introduced ''Bohemian Rhapsody'' * [[Bill Murray]] with Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy * [[Gary Oldman]] with Best Actress – Motion Picture Drama * [[Richard Gere]] and [[Julianne Moore]] with Best Actor – Motion Picture Drama * [[Nicole Kidman]] with Best Motion Picture – Drama ==Broadcast== For the first time, NBC televised the 4:40 p.m. [[Eastern Time Zone|EST]] Sunday afternoon [[2018–19 NFL playoffs|NFL Wild Card playoff]] game (which has historically gone to another [[National Football League|NFL]] broadcast partner such as [[Fox Broadcasting Company|Fox]]) as a lead-in to the Golden Globes. Because of the large viewership of NFL playoff games, this was expected to boost the Golden Globes' TV ratings, which dropped 11% between 2017 and 2018.{{cite web|url=https://www.thewrap.com/golden-globes-are-already-ratings-hit-nfl-playoffs/|title=Why the 2019 Golden Globes Are Already a Ratings Hit – Probably|website=The Wrap|first1=Tony|last1=Maglio|first2=Tim|last2=Baysinger|date=January 3, 2019|access-date=January 5, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190106055124/https://www.thewrap.com/golden-globes-are-already-ratings-hit-nfl-playoffs/|archive-date=January 6, 2019|url-status=live}} Despite the NFL lead-in, the Golden Globes achieved a lower household rating and less total viewers than the 2018 ceremony, though the show experienced a slight increase in the 18–49 demographic.{{cite web|url=https://variety.com/2019/tv/news/golden-globes-ratings-2019-1203100895/|title=Golden Globes Ratings 2019|website=Variety|first1=Joe|last1=Otterson|date=January 7, 2019|access-date=August 14, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190814035304/https://variety.com/2019/tv/news/golden-globes-ratings-2019-1203100895/|archive-date=August 14, 2019|url-status=live}} The regular Golden Globes Red Carpet Preshow was livestreamed exclusively on [[Facebook Watch]] instead of airing on NBC.{{cite web|url=https://www.adweek.com/digital/the-golden-globes-red-carpet-preshow-will-livestream-exclusively-on-facebook-watch/|title=The Golden Globes Red Carpet Preshow Will Livestream Exclusively on Facebook Watch|website=Adweek|first=David|last=Cohen|date=January 4, 2019|access-date=January 5, 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190106011143/https://www.adweek.com/digital/the-golden-globes-red-carpet-preshow-will-livestream-exclusively-on-facebook-watch/|archive-date=January 6, 2019|url-status=live}} ==In Memoriam== No "In Memoriam" section was broadcast on television during the ceremony, so the HFPA included a slideshow on their website,{{cite magazine |url=https://ew.com/golden-globes/2019/01/06/golden-globes-2019-in-memoriam/ |title=Why the Golden Globes doesn't have an In Memoriam segment |first=Maureen Lee |last=Lenker |date=January 6, 2019 |access-date=January 7, 2019 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |publisher=[[Meredith Corporation]]
